betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Manufactory§f:
The Manufactory (nuclearcraft:manufactory) is a single-block machine that processes raw materials into useful components. It is the most basic and earliest NC machine you should build.

§6Slot Layout (ManufactoryRecipes.java:18)§f:
1 item input → 0 fluid input → 1 item output → 0 fluid output. The Manufactory takes one stack and produces one transformed stack.

§6Crafting Recipe (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:67)§f:
Pattern "LRL / FPF / LSL" in a 3×3 grid. Letters: L = ingotLead, R = dustRedstone, F = Items.FLINT, P = vanilla Piston, S = solenoidCopper. Yields 1 Manufactory.

§6What It Does (ManufactoryRecipes.java)§f:
The Manufactory is the "kitchen sink" processor for early-game recipes:
- Cobblestone → Gravel
- Gravel → Sand
- Sand → Dust (via enrichment)
- Various ore → dust equivalents
- Basic material compaction (dust → ingot when not covered by Ingot Former)
- Universal dust/ingot/nugget conversions for missing OreDict entries
- Recovery of rare dusts from common ores (chance-based)

§6Direct Descendants of Manufactory (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The graph shows 7 items used in the Manufactory recipe:
- ingotLead (thermalfoundation, nuclearcraft, hbm all map to the same OreDict)
- dustRedstone (minecraft)
- Items.FLINT (minecraft)
- Blocks.PISTON (minecraft)
- solenoidCopper (nuclearcraft:part:4)

§6Role in NC Tech Tree§f:
The Manufactory is the §esecond NC machine you unlock§f (after the Alloy Furnace). It is the entry point for:
- Converting Cobblestone to Gravel to Sand for early concrete
- Processing raw ores into dusts for the Ingot Former
- Producing ingots from dusts as a fallback path

§6Synergy With Ingot Former§f:
The Manufactory and Ingot Former are complementary. Use:
- §bManufactory§f: dust → ingot (one type, no fluid form)
- §bIngot Former§f: dust → ingot using fluid metal (allows recycling of fluid outputs)

§6Prerequisites§f:
Requires q30021 (Alloy Furnace) — the basic NC machine unlock.

§eTip§f: Place a row of 4-8 Manufactories with speed upgrades. They are cheap to build and consume little power, so overproduction is always useful.

§eTip§f: Use the Manufactory to convert early-game Cobblestone into Sand, then Sand into dust, then dust into Silicon via QMD's processing chain. This avoids the need for an external sand-to-dust recipe.

§cWarning§f: The Manufactory cannot process most late-game materials. For those, use the Ingot Former, Assembler, or Chemical Reactor instead.
